methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $350M total raised proxy.

methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Oxford Quantum Circuits is a Series C quantum computing company that has raised $350M to date. The valuation comp universe comprises 11 peers across quantum computing—3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion—though narrow peer filtering by stage and valuation tier yields insufficient datapoints for reliable median comparison.

Of the two triangulation methods applied, only stage-progression analysis is applicable. This method uses the $350M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement to the next funding stage, yielding $595M. The peer-median approach cannot be reliably applied; while 5 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the criteria of matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier simultaneously (minimum 3 required), making an all-sector median comparison potentially misleading given the heterogeneous maturity and technology positioning within quantum computing.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $506M to $684M, with a median point of $595M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of comparable company analysis due to sparse same-stage peer data and the reliance on raised capital as a valuation proxy rather than market-based metrics. A broader Dataset incorporating more adjacent-stage or cross-sector deep-tech comps would strengthen conviction in this range.
